  1. Home
  2. Defense
  3. Military Rotorcraft

MH-139 Grey Wolf

Utility versatility for many missions

The MH-139 is a multimission utility helicopter produced in the United States. The aircraft incorporates state-of-the-art technology that allows operators to complete any mission safely, quickly and efficiently.

Capabilities and Features

Mission Flexibility

The MH-139’s multimission capability enables the execution of a variety of missions, including patrol, firefighting, search and rescue, VIP, troop and cargo transport.
Rapid response and maritime patrol

Maintains rapid response and effective search-and-surveillance capabilities vital for operators across the military, law enforcement, maritime/border patrol and security missions in all weather conditions.

Modular cabin and seating

Flexible configurations allow seating for up to 11 people.

Integrated avionics and communications

Operators gain maximum benefit from the comprehensive communications suite.

Safe and Secure

Modern capabilities make the MH-139 a safe and effective platform for a variety of mission sets.
FAA-certified platform

The safety pedigree of the MH-139 is based on Federal Aviation Administration certification.

Crashworthy design features

The aircraft contains crashworthy fuel tanks and seats, bird strike protection, and dual-hydraulic, electric, main gearbox and engine lubrication systems.

Enhanced survivability modifications

Modified military installations add capability and improve survivability.

Refined proficiency

The advanced flight deck functionality improves situational awareness, resulting in reduced pilot and crew workload.

Secure avionics integration

With encrypted avionics and radio integration capabilities, the aircraft is well suited to perform military and homeland security missions.

Sustainment and Training

A proven baseline design, a worldwide supply chain, and established pilot and maintainer training knowledge help make the MH-139 a cost-effective solution.
Blended systems

World-class military and commercial integration capabilities.

Commonality in training

An integrated training system based on existing AW139 operational flight trainers.

Time-saving mechanisms

Automated sustainment tools to allow seamless USG interfaces.

Global support and parts

Worldwide commercial/DOD support system and parts procurement.

On-the-spot care

A unique design that enables maintainers to accomplish all aircraft maintenance at the operational level.

Established installed base

Fleet of more than 1,000 aircraft for guaranteed long-term support.

Specifications

Rotor Diameter45 feet, 3 inches (13.8 meters)
Height16 feet, 4 inches  (5 meters)
Max Cruising Speed146 knots
Max Range
410 nautical miles
Length54 feet, 8 inches (16.7 meters)
Max Takeoff Weight15,432 pounds (7,000 kilograms)
Max Speed167 knots
Service Ceiling20,000 feet (6,096 meters)

Customers

The U.S. Air Force acquired the MH-139 to monitor intercontinental ballistic missiles and transport U.S. government officials and security forces.

(Photo/U.S. Air Force)

Industry Team

Leonardo Helicopters

The strength of the MH-139 lies in the efforts of Leonardo, as an original equipment manufacturer, and Boeing, as prime contractor. By offering a proven AW139 platform and integrating additional military capabilities into the MH-139, customers get the best product via an existing industry partner, configurable for a variety of mission sets, at the best value.

News

The MH-139A will patrol nuclear missile silos across the Midwest. (Boeing Photo)
Boeing to Build Four Additional MH-139A Helicopters for U.S. Air Force
Read more
A U.S. Air Force MH-139A Grey Wolf assigned to the 40th Helicopter Squadron conducts its first operational mission at Malmstrom Air Force Base, Montana, Jan. 8, 2026. The mission marks the beginning of the replacement of the Vietnam-era UH-1N Huey and represents a key step in modernizing security for the nation’s land-based nuclear deterrent. (U.S. Air Force photo by Airman 1st Class Teniya Caldwell)
MH-139 Grey Wolf helicopters complete first operational ICBM convoy security mission
Read more
Boeing secures contract for 8 more Grey Wolf helicopters
Read more
Grey Wolf testing shows it can fly its mission
Read more
Photos: Grey Wolf tested for US Air Force mission
MH-139 trainers delivered to Maxwell Air Force Base
Boeing delivers 1st MH-139A production aircraft to US Air Force
MH-139 Grey Wolf
Boeing Delivers Four MH-139A Test Helicopters to U.S. Air Force

Air Force names newest helicopter ‘Grey Wolf’
Read more
Boeing MH-139 to Replace U.S. Air Force UH-1N Huey Fleet
Read more
Boeing Launches Proven, Affordable MH-139 in U.S. Air Force Helicopter Competition
Read more
Boeing delivers 4 MH-139 test helicopters to US Air Force

Video

Program Highlights

The MH-139 has industry-leading speed and range for medium utility helicopters.
The MH-139 maintains VIP transport capabilities, particularly in urban environments.
An MH-139 makes an austere landing in a grass field.
